documentation.value: Writes customized output to the host (console). Supports colored text output. Note: output is not sent to the pipeline.
The object to display. Can be a string, number, or any object.
Suppresses the newline at the end of the output.
Text color: Black, DarkBlue, DarkGreen, DarkCyan, DarkRed, DarkMagenta, DarkYellow, Gray, DarkGray, Blue, Green, Cyan, Red, Magenta, Yellow, White.
Background color. Same color options as ForegroundColor.
Sends the specified objects to the next command in the pipeline. If it is the last command, the objects are displayed in the console.
The objects to send through the pipeline.
By default, collections are enumerated. This switch suppresses that behavior.
Gets the items and child items in one or more specified locations (files, folders, registry keys, etc.).
The path(s) to get child items from. Wildcards are permitted.
A filter in the provider's format (e.g., '*.txt'). More efficient than -Include.
Gets items in all child containers (subdirectories).
String array of patterns to include (e.g., '*.log', '*.txt').
String array of patterns to exclude.
Sets the current working location to a specified location (directory, registry key, etc.).
The path to the new location.
Returns the PathInfo object representing the location.
Creates an instance of a .NET or COM object.
The fully qualified name of the .NET class (e.g., 'System.Collections.ArrayList').
Arguments to pass to the constructor.
A hashtable of property names and values to set on the new object.
Sends an HTTP or HTTPS request to a web page or web service and parses the response.
The URI of the web resource to send the request to.
The HTTP method: Default, Get, Head, Post, Put, Delete, Trace, Options, Merge, Patch.
The body of the request. Can be a string, hashtable, or byte array.
A dictionary of additional HTTP headers.
The content type of the request (e.g., 'application/json').
Saves the response body to the specified file.
Converts an object to a JSON-formatted string.
The object to convert to JSON.
The maximum depth of nested objects to include. Default is 2.
Omits whitespace and indentation for a compact representation.
Converts a JSON-formatted string to a PowerShell object (PSCustomObject or Hashtable).
The JSON string to convert.
Converts JSON to a Hashtable instead of PSCustomObject.
Maximum depth the JSON input is allowed to have. Default is 1024.
Performs an operation against each item in a collection of input objects.
The script block to run for each input object. Use $_ or $PSItem for the current object.
The input objects. Usually received from the pipeline.
Script block to run before processing any input objects.
Script block to run after processing all input objects.
Selects objects from a collection based on their property values.
The script block used to filter objects. Returns $true to include the object (e.g., { $_.Length -gt 100 }).
The objects to filter. Usually received from the pipeline.
Selects specified properties of objects or a specified number of objects.
The property names to select (e.g., Name, Length, @{Name='Size';Expression={$_.Length}}).
Number of objects to select from the beginning.
Number of objects to select from the end.
A property to expand (select all items in the property's collection).
Sorts objects by property values.
The property or properties to sort by.
Sorts in descending order.
Eliminates duplicates and returns only unique members.
Determines whether all elements of a path exist. Returns $true or $false.
The path to test. Wildcards are permitted.
The type of element to test: Any, Container (directory), Leaf (file).
Tests whether the path syntax is valid, regardless of whether the path exists.
Gets the content of a file as an array of strings (one per line) or as a single string with -Raw.
The path to the file(s) to read.
Read only the first N lines.
Read only the last N lines.
The encoding: ASCII, UTF8, UTF7, UTF32, Unicode, BigEndianUnicode, Default, OEM.
Returns the entire file as a single string instead of an array of lines.
